name: verify
context: Verify deployed contract source code on Etherscan, Basescan, or Blockscout block explorers
info: Source verification publishes your Solidity source and compiler settings to a block explorer so anyone can read the contract, confirm it matches the deployed bytecode, and interact via the explorer UI. Verification requires the exact compiler version, optimization settings, and constructor arguments used during deployment. Both Hardhat and Foundry support automatic verification with `--verify`. Manual verification is available via the Etherscan API or web interface. Verified contracts show a green checkmark and enable the Read/Write Contract tabs on the explorer.
triggers: how do i verify a contract
body:
# Contract Verification

Verification proves that the deployed bytecode matches the published source. It enables the Read/Write Contract tabs on block explorers. This step should follow every production deployment via [[ethereum/contract-deploy]].

## Hardhat

```bash
npx hardhat verify --network base CONTRACT_ADDRESS "constructor_arg_1" "constructor_arg_2"
```

Or use `--verify` flag during Ignition deployment (see [[ethereum/contract-deploy/hardhat#deploy-command]]).

## Foundry

```bash
forge verify-contract CONTRACT_ADDRESS src/MyContract.sol:MyContract --chain base --etherscan-api-key $ETHERSCAN_API_KEY
```

Or use `--verify` flag during `forge script` (see [[ethereum/contract-deploy/foundry#forge-script]]).

## Requirements

- Exact Solidity compiler version
- Same optimization settings (runs count, via-ir)
- ABI-encoded constructor arguments
- Etherscan API key for the target explorer

## Explorer API Keys

| Explorer | Chains | API Key Source |
|----------|--------|----------------|
| Etherscan | Ethereum mainnet, Sepolia | etherscan.io/apis |
| Basescan | Base, Base Sepolia | basescan.org/apis |
| Arbiscan | Arbitrum | arbiscan.io/apis |
| Blockscout | Various L2s | Self-hosted, no key needed |

## Troubleshooting

Common failures: wrong compiler version, different optimization settings, missing constructor args, library linking mismatch. Check `artifacts/` or `out/` for the exact build settings used during deployment.

sections.title: Verify with Hardhat
sections.body: `npx hardhat verify --network <name> <address> <args>`. Requires `@nomicfoundation/hardhat-verify` plugin and `etherscan.apiKey` in config (see [[ethereum/contract-deploy/hardhat#setup]]). Supports custom explorer URLs for L2s and Blockscout.

sections.title: Verify with Foundry
sections.body: `forge verify-contract <address> <contract> --chain <chain> --etherscan-api-key <key>`. Add `--constructor-args $(cast abi-encode "constructor(type)" value)` for contracts with constructor arguments.

sections.title: Troubleshooting verification
sections.body: Verification fails when compiler version, optimization runs, or constructor args don't match the deployment. Check build artifacts for exact settings. For proxy contracts (see [[ethereum/contract-deploy#deployment-patterns]]), verify both the implementation and proxy separately.

related.note: A contract must be deployed before it can be verified

scripts.name: Verify Contract
scripts.description: Verify a deployed contract on a block explorer

explorerUrl.description: Link to verified contract on explorer

author: dojo-community
